📖 English

Use of Adverbs

Words that tell us how, when, where, and why!

📚 Chapter 5 ⏱️ 1 min read

Unit 5: Use of Adverbs

An adverb modifies (gives extra information about) a verb, an adjective, or another adverb.

Example: He runs quickly. (tells how he runs)

Types of Adverbs

TypeWhat it tellsExamples
MannerHow?slowly, carefully, happily
PlaceWhere?here, there, everywhere
TimeWhen?today, yesterday, now
FrequencyHow often?always, often, never
DegreeHow much/extent?very, too, quite
Reason/PurposeWhy?therefore, hence, thus
InterrogativeAsks questionwhy, when, where, how
SentenceModifies whole sentencefortunately, honestly

Detailed Examples

Adverbs of Manner

She sings beautifully.

Adverbs of Place

Please sit here.

Adverbs of Time

I will call you tomorrow.

Adverbs of Frequency

He always brushes his teeth.

Adverbs of Degree

The soup is too hot.

Adverbs of Reason

It was raining; therefore we stayed home.

Sentence Adverbs

Fortunately, nobody was hurt.

Interrogative Adverbs

Why are you late? Where is my book?

💡 Tip: Many adverbs end in -ly (quickly, softly), but not all (fast, very, now).

Try It Yourself!

  1. Find the adverb: “She spoke softly.”
  2. Identify the type: “He never lies.”
  3. Fill in the blank: ”______, we reached on time. (Luckily/Nice)”

Adverbs add precision and detail. They answer the little questions like how, when, and where!

🎯
